creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Aleks from comment #11)

I'm confused. Your original submittal was for starting from 10.1-Release.
Does your new note mean that you have updated to:

FreeBSD 11.1-STABLE #0 r332428

?

I've no clue what your goals are to be picking what version that you
might want to update to.

You can view the release dates and expire dates for old releases at:

https://www.freebsd.org/security/unsupported.html

and release dates and any planned expire dates at:

https://www.freebsd.org/security/

With these you can do your own time-overlap analysis if you want to work
that way.

For the entries stable/12 and stable/11 you could consider the release dates
to be the same as 12.0-REELEASE and, say, 11.2-RELEASE, respectively, for
picking something based on supported releases. Then pick on or after the matching
-r?????? .

As for what -r?????? is for release/11.2.0 (for example):

https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/release/11.2.0/?view=log

shows -r335563 . So stable/12 on or after that.

For release/12.0.0 :

https://svnweb.freebsd.org/base/release/12.0.0/?view=log

shows -r341707 . So stable/12 on or after that.

So yu can find such -r?????? information looking in such places.

(Personally I wish more materials showed -r?????? information in addition to
dates for releases.)

If you are at some 10.x or 11.x variant, jumping to some 12.0 variant gets
back into the cross-major-version issues where the date-range overlap is more
likely to be appropriate.

I'll note that I've assumed you are using a more typical architecture, say amd64.
Sometimes familiues, say, the 32-bit powerpc families, have more issues for how
to go forward.
